patch.value: Perf: pm context / pm next hot path ~700ms on an ~850-item tracker

From 2026-07-02 audit (Lane D, pm-3l76): node startup is ~60ms, but pm context --limit 5 and pm next take ~700ms warm on this repo (857 items); list-open ~430ms, get ~335ms. Dominant cost is full-corpus load + actionability scan. Candidates: reuse/extend the front-matter cache on the context/next path, lazy-load bodies, avoid re-parsing closed items for ready-leaf computation. Target: common reads under ~300ms.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Implementation evidence: CPU-profiled pm next on this repo (857 items, ~1450 toon files). Dominant costs: (1) each runList re-read+re-parsed metadata-cache.json (3.2MB) and metadata-cache-collections.json (4.9MB) — pm next/context call runList twice, so ~16MB JSON per invocation; (2) Date.parse per comparison in compareTimestampStrings and regex pipeline in normalizeStatusToken inside O(n log n) sort comparators. Fixes: stat-validated in-process memo for the three cache envelopes in front-matter-cache.ts (invalidated on persist + by stat signature; also removes per-call re-parse in the long-lived MCP server), plus capped memos for Date.parse and status-token normalization. Warm timings before→after: next 1282-1545→564-699ms, context 1233-1579→494-703ms, list --status open 987-1042→425-478ms, get 704-762→262-304ms. Behavior verified byte-identical vs published 2026.7.2 on this tracker (next/list/get identical; context differs only in embedded now timestamps). Remaining above-300ms cost for next/context is ~300ms ESM module load + double corpus stat-scan — candidates for a follow-up build-level lazy-load pass.

patch.value: Merged PR #421: stat-validated in-process memo for the three front-matter cache envelopes (LRU re-insert on hit, cap 24, repopulated on persist) plus capped memos for Date.parse in compareTimestampStrings and normalizeStatusToken; shared evictOldestMemoEntries half-eviction helper in core/shared/memo.ts.
Common reads (pm next/context/list/get) substantially faster warm with byte-identical output; target under ~300ms for common reads.

Warm on this 857-item tracker: next 1282-1545->564-699ms, context 1233-1579->494-703ms, list --status open 987-1042->425-478ms, get 704-762->262-304ms. get meets the 300ms target; next/context remaining cost is ~300ms ESM bundle load + double corpus stat-scan (build-level lazy-load follow-up candidate). Output verified byte-identical vs published 2026.7.2; 5283 tests 100% coverage; greptile 2 local rounds + PR bots (greptile/gemini/coderabbit) all clean or addressed.

Merged PR #421: stat-validated in-process memo for the three front-matter cache envelopes (LRU re-insert on hit, cap 24, repopulated on persist) plus capped memos for Date.parse in compareTimestampStrings and normalizeStatusToken; shared evictOldestMemoEntries half-eviction helper in core/shared/memo.ts.
